page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the n4 family. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 1 physical Intel core with 2 threads. The processor includes an L1d cache of 48 KB, an L2 cache of 2 MB, and a large L3 cache of 260 MB. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities but weaker multi-core and memory performance. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score is 1657.98, and the Geekbench single-core score is 1836.0, both falling into the average tier. However, multi-core benchmarks are limited, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 1736.79 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 2242.0. Memory bandwidth is a notable constraint, with large-block read speeds at 17.81 GB/sec and write speeds at 11.0 GB/sec, both of which are weak.

The hardware profile presents clear tradeoffs. While the CPU supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512, the low memory capacity of 4.0 GB and weak memory bandwidth restrict its utility for memory-intensive tasks. Database operations and Redis SET performance are low, with Redis achieving 2.31 million operations per second. This instance is best suited for lightweight, compute-bound workloads that benefit from dedicated single-core execution and vector instructions but do not require large memory footprints, such as basic text processing, single-threaded utility scripts, or lightweight web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs from a single physical Intel core and 4.0 GB of RAM. Lacking bundled local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance relies on its Intel processor, which includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmark data reveals average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1836.0, but weak multi-core and memory bandwidth results. The 2.0 GB of RAM per core ratio represents a clear tradeoff, making the instance less suitable for memory-heavy databases or complex multi-threaded tasks. It is best positioned for lightweight, compute-focused workloads, single-threaded applications, basic text processing, and low-traffic web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 server with 2 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM. It offers average single-core performance but limited memory bandwidth for lightweight compute workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 instance with 2 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM, designed for lightweight compute tasks.
tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU compute instance with advanced Intel instruction support, optimized for lightweight single-threaded tasks and basic processing workloads.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 4.0 GB of system memory
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 advanced instruction sets
Average single-core performance with 260 MB L3 cache
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for lightweight web serving and single-threaded processing
